quote_of_day.text:

>revenge
for what yaar? it is not like she abused me or anything, i was just abandoned, I don't even remember much of it.
It is just that my grandparents have passed away and I am now an official anaath, it feels sad sometimes.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of body dysmorphia moved into the board's Leading section, as an anon sought advice on looksmaxxing surgery to alleviate concerns about their physique. This drew a flurry of responses, mostly speculative about surgical options and societal pressures.

A cold front of disillusionment swept through the Discourse section, as an anon lamented the mainstreaming of once-niche anime, sparking a brief but heated exchange about cultural ownership and nostalgia.

Meanwhile, a separate thread in Discourse featured a somber drizzle of regret, as an anon reflected on a life marked by abandonment and hardship.

In the Notices section, a series of scattered showers of curiosity washed over two threads: one regarding the demise of Project Bharatchan, with anon speculating about its implications, and another revisiting Indiachan's past, marked by brief, disjointed comments.

articles.title: Anon Seeks Surgery for 'Looksmaxxing'
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread expressing frustration with finding clothes that fit due to wide hips and seeking advice on looksmaxxing, a term associated with self-improvement for aesthetic purposes.

The original poster, a man, asked about surgical options to address what he perceives as a flaw in his body shape. One respondent suggested that the OP consider calisthenics, noting however that it requires a significant initial investment of effort.

Another anon provided a nutritional guideline, stating that a person weighing 70 kilograms should consume around 77 grams of complete protein daily, which can be achieved through a diet including half a kilogram of chicken.

A different approach was offered by an anon who advised the OP, who is 178 cm tall and weighs 76 kg, that his body measurements do not indicate a significant issue with weight or underweight status.

This anon suggested incorporating walking and a few pushups into his routine, recommending against rushing into gym workouts. The conversation also touched on the topic of gym culture and safety.

One anon expressed frustration with what he perceived as irrational fear of gym injuries, suggesting that watching YouTube videos on proper lifting techniques could mitigate risks.

In contrast, another anon shared a personal experience of being discouraged from working out by family members, reflecting a broader societal attitude. Anon also discussed the challenges of dietary and exercise regimens in the context of economic and familial pressures.

One participant pointed out that not everyone has the financial means to pursue fitness goals easily, highlighting the costs associated with both gym memberships and proper nutrition. The discussion revealed a range of perspectives on body image, fitness, and self-improvement.

While some respondents offered practical advice on diet and exercise, others shared personal anecdotes and observations about societal attitudes toward physical fitness and appearance.

articles.title: Anon Laments Mainstreaming of Niche Anime
articles.body:
Anon expressed frustration about niche anime becoming mainstream. The original poster claimed to have been ahead of others in internet culture, watching niche anime since they were 9 years old.

Anon felt that when a series gets too popular, it attracts the wrong kind of audience. One respondent cited the example of "Mushoku Tensei" and how Indian feminists reacted to it.

Another poster shared a similar sentiment, blaming "foids" for ruining niche interests by making them mainstream. Some anons reminisced about the past when niche anime was not widely known.

One poster mentioned that even in 2012, they heard people in their private school talking about popular anime like "Naruto" and "One Piece." However, another respondent noted that these are not niche anime and that true niche anime, like "Claymore," has been negatively impacted by the influx of new fans.

The discussion also touched on the changing demographics of anime fans. One poster mentioned that now "every single randi is into anime," making it harder for them to find like-minded people.

Some anons expressed a desire to distance themselves from the mainstreaming of anime and its associated culture. The conversation was marked by a sense of nostalgia for a time when niche interests were not widely known.

Anons also lamented the loss of anonymity and the impact it has on online communities. One poster noted that 4chan, a platform known for anonymity, has lost its meaning over time.

articles.title: Abandoned at 6, poster reflects on life alone
articles.body:
Anon shared their personal story of abandonment, having lost their father in a road accident at a young age and subsequently being raised by their grandparents.

After their grandparents passed away, the poster is now on their own, having previously tried to reconnect with their mother to no avail.

One respondent suggested the poster consider leaving India, stating that there is little keeping them in the country and that navigating life as a solo player in India is nearly impossible.

Another anon countered this advice, pointing out that the poster has managed to secure a job and pay off their education loan, allowing them to settle in a tier 2 city.

The poster reflected on their situation, stating they have accepted their circumstances and do not feel overly resentful. They expressed a sense of freedom in not being tied down to family obligations.

Some respondents offered words of encouragement, suggesting the poster focus on the positive aspects of their life and let go of any desires for revenge or vengeance. However, not all responses were supportive.

Some anons offered brutal advice, including suggestions of violent actions against the poster's mother. Others made disparaging remarks, calling the poster a "cuck" for not harboring strong resentment.

The poster's occupation was revealed to be in the ITeS sector, earning 7 LPA, which has allowed them to achieve some stability in their life.

Despite the challenges, the poster seems to be seeking solace in their online community, having previously shared their story on another platform when their grandmother passed away.

articles.title: Anon Speculates on Project Bharatchan's Demise
articles.body:
Anon kicked off a discussion by questioning what was meant by "Project Bharatchan is over." One anon suggested that the project's end might be due to its participants becoming "bhosdapilled" and no longer useful.

Another anon mentioned that Project Bharatchan was a tie-up between Doval and the NSA.

Anon also floated the idea that the project was shut down because its users, described as "retards," were no longer manageable and risked damaging India's image globally.

The project's apparent demise sparked speculation about the motivations of its creators and the implications for India's online presence.

articles.title: Anon Discusses Indiachan's Past
articles.body: Anon started a thread with a brief statement about "Dayush". Another anon responded, mentioning that Indiachan went downhill after certain groups lost control, and made a comment about Jewish influence. A respondent stated that Indiachan was dominated by Kshatriya chads after someone named Rusty. Anon also discussed the current state of Indiachan, with one stating the current owner is "lungimoot" and that the userbase is problematic.
